title: Charmed Life - Chapter 17 (Fragment 5)
text:
“No,” said Cat. “Just see. Or he’d know all about you. I’m glad you’re staying behind. We can go and get the dragons’ blood while they’re at church.”

Janet kept watch at the window to see when the Family left. After about half an hour, she said, “Here they are at last, walking in a crowd down the avenue. All the men have got toppers, but Chrestomanci looks as if he’s come out of a shop window. Who are they all, Cat? Who’s the old lady in purple mittens, and the young one in green, and the little fellow who’s always talking?”

“I’ve no idea,” said Cat. He scrambled out of bed and scuttled up to his room to find some clothes. He felt perfectly well—marvelously well, in fact. He danced around his room while he put on his shirt. He sang putting on his trousers. Even the cold lump of dough on the carpet could not damp his spirits. He whistled tying his boots.

Janet came into the room as Cat was shooting out of it, pulling on his jacket and beaming with health. “I don’t know,” Janet said, as Cat shot past her and hammered away down the stairs. “Dying must agree with you, or something.”

“Hurry up!” Cat called from the bottom of the stairs. “It’s on the other side of the Castle from here. Millie says dragons’ blood is very dangerous, so don’t you touch it. I can spare a life on it and you can’t.”

Janet wanted to remark that Cat had not spared the last one very easily, but she never caught up with Cat sufficiently. Cat whirled through the green corridors and stormed up the winding stairs to Mr. Saunders’ room, and Janet only reached him when he was actually inside it. Then there was too much else to take up her attention.

The room was heavy with the scent of stale magic. Though it was much the same as when Cat had seen it before, Mr. Saunders had tidied it a little for Sunday. The cresset was out. The torts and limbecks and other vessels were all clean. The books and scrolls had been piled in heaps on the second bench. The five-pointed star was still there, blazoned on the floor, but there was a new set of signs chalked on the third bench, and the mummified animal had been neatly laid at one end of it.

Janet was immensely interested. “It’s like a laboratory,” she said, “except that it isn’t. What weird things! Oh, I see the dragons’ blood. Does he need all that huge jar? He won’t miss a bit out of that lot.”

There was a rustling at the end of the third bench. Janet’s head perked towards it. The mummified creature was twitching and spreading its filmy little wings.

“It did that before,” said Cat. “I think it’s all right.”

He was not so sure, however, when the creature stretched and got to its doglike feet, yawning. The yawn showed them dozens of small, sharp teeth and also let out a cloud of bluish smoke. The creature ran pattering along the bench towards them. The little wings rattled on its back as it came, and two small puffs of smoke streamed behind it from its nostrils. It stopped at the edge of the bench to look up at them inquisitively from a melting glitter of golden eyes. They backed nervously away from it.

vocabulary.definition: A broad road in a town or city, often lined with trees.
vocabulary.example: Janet notices people walking in a crowd down the avenue.

vocabulary.definition: To smile radiantly.
vocabulary.example: Cat was smiling brightly as he dressed.

vocabulary.definition: A long table used for work, especially in a laboratory or workshop.
vocabulary.example: Work surfaces held books and materials.

vocabulary.definition: To display prominently or vividly; to decorate with heraldic bearings.
vocabulary.example: A symbol was prominently displayed on the floor.

vocabulary.definition: To write or draw on a surface with chalk.
vocabulary.example: Symbols were drawn on a surface using chalk.

vocabulary.definition: A metal cup or basket holding a light, often mounted on a pole or wall.
vocabulary.example: The room had a metal lamp that was not lit.

vocabulary.definition: To lessen someone's enthusiasm or spirits.
vocabulary.example: Despite problems, Cat remained cheerful and lively.

vocabulary.definition: Resembling a thin film; delicate and translucent.
vocabulary.example: The creature had delicate, thin wings.

vocabulary.definition: To do something more quickly; to move faster.
vocabulary.example: Cat urged Janet to be quick.

vocabulary.definition: To an exceedingly great extent or degree.
vocabulary.example: Janet showed a very high level of interest.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows curiosity or a desire to learn.
vocabulary.example: The creature looked at them with curious interest.

vocabulary.definition: A room or building equipped for scientific experiments, research, or teaching.
vocabulary.example: The room resembled a space for scientific work.

vocabulary.definition: A type of alembic (distillation apparatus) traditionally used in alchemy or chemistry.
vocabulary.example: Laboratory vessels were prepared neatly for use.

vocabulary.definition: Preserved from decay, especially as a corpse wrapped in bandages.
vocabulary.example: A preserved creature was placed carefully.

vocabulary.definition: To make a series of light tapping sounds.
vocabulary.example: The creature made soft tapping sounds as it moved.

vocabulary.definition: To make a rapid succession of short sharp knocking sounds.
vocabulary.example: The creature's wings made a shaking sound.

vocabulary.definition: A soft, muffled crackling sound like that caused by the movement of dry leaves or paper.
vocabulary.example: A quiet sound was heard from the bench.

vocabulary.definition: To move hurriedly or clumsily from one place or position to another.
vocabulary.example: Cat quickly got out of bed and moved to his room.

vocabulary.definition: To move hurriedly with short quick steps.
vocabulary.example: Cat moved quickly upstairs with short steps.

vocabulary.definition: To refrain from harming or damaging; to save or give up something.
vocabulary.example: Cat says he can afford to lose a life if necessary.

vocabulary.definition: No longer fresh; musty or unpleasant from standing.
vocabulary.example: The magic in the room seemed old or heavy.

vocabulary.definition: To move angrily or forcefully somewhere.
vocabulary.example: Cat forcefully ran up the stairs.

vocabulary.definition: A tall, cylindrical hat traditionally worn by men.
vocabulary.example: Men in the story are described as wearing toppers.

vocabulary.definition: A vessel used for distillation or other chemical processes.
vocabulary.example: Laboratory glassware was clean and tidy.

vocabulary.definition: To make a sudden, small movement.
vocabulary.example: The preserved creature made small movements.

vocabulary.definition: To move or cause to move rapidly around and around.
vocabulary.example: Cat moved quickly and energetically through corridors.

vocabulary.definition: To open the mouth wide and breathe in deeply, typically through tiredness or boredom.
vocabulary.example: The creature opened its mouth wide in a yawn.
